creator: marco@crowdsec.net
text:
(In reply to Alexey Dokuchaev from comment #1)


> We could do that, but all existing Abseil consumers work with dynamic libraries just fine.  Can't `security/crowdsec' be taught to eat them as well?

Oh, it works well enough with dynamic linking, but makes it harder to provide testing packages for pfsense and opnsense for example. Now I need to build all the libraries against the right port trees and distribute them as well.

> Alternative is to add `static' flavor which other ports can depend on, but honestly, good software should link common libraries dynamically these days.

I'm not calling the shots and understand the rationale, but the whole go ecosystem is very biased towards static linking.

What I'll do, is maintain these - ugly - abseil changes, or a static flavor, in my port repository, so that it's possible to build them statically outside of the upstream tree.

Thanks for your review!
